name: Charminar
description: The 430-year-old icon of Hyderabad. Four minarets, chaotic bangle bazaars, and the best people-watching in the city.
sid: Go on a weekday evening. Eat at Nimrah Café right opposite — Irani chai + Osmania biscuits for ₹60 while staring at the Charminar. Unbeatable.

name: Golconda Fort
description: Massive 16th-century fort with acoustics so good a clap at the gate is heard at the top. The sunset view from the summit is the best free thing in Hyderabad.
bestTime: 4 PM, catch sunset at the top
sid: 380 steps. Wear real shoes, not chappals. Clap at the entrance dome — a guard will demo the acoustics if you ask.

name: Hussain Sagar & Tank Bund
description: The giant heart-shaped lake with the Buddha statue in the middle. Tank Bund at night is the city's default hangout stretch.
sid: Take the ₹55 boat to the Buddha statue once, just to say you did. Then get corn from the carts and walk the Bund.

name: Salar Jung Museum
description: One of the world's largest one-man collections. The Veiled Rebecca alone is worth the trip. The musical clock draws a crowd at every hour.
sid: Be at the clock room 5 minutes before any hour. Combine with Charminar — they're 10 minutes apart.

name: Khajaguda Rocks
description: 2.5-billion-year-old granite boulders 15 minutes from campus. Sunrise scrambles, sunset views over the city, and full-moon night climbs.
sid: THE classic IIITH escape. Closest real nature to campus. Go with seniors the first time — the good viewpoint isn't the obvious one.

name: Durgam Cheruvu
description: The 'Secret Lake' with the glowing cable bridge. Lakefront promenade, rock formations, and the bridge lit up purple at night.
sid: Walk the cable bridge after dark, then descend to the promenade. Cheapest good date in the city.

name: Lamakaan
description: An open cultural space in an old bungalow — plays, standup, film screenings, poetry, most of it free. Dosa and chai at canteen prices.
sid: The most 'how did I not know about this' place in Hyderabad. Check lamakaan.com before going — the event makes the visit.

name: Shilparamam
description: Crafts village next to Hitec City — handicraft stalls, night bazaar during festivals, and a surprisingly peaceful rock garden.
sid: Go during Sankranti or Dasara when the night bazaar is on. Good cheap gift shopping before going home for holidays.

name: KBR National Park
description: 390 acres of actual forest in the middle of the richest neighbourhood in the city. Peacocks everywhere at dawn.
sid: Morning walk here + breakfast at Chutneys Jubilee Hills after. Certified reset-your-brain combo during exam season.

name: Shah Ghouse
description: The biryani that settles arguments. Also does haleem in Ramzan that people cross the city for. Open till late.
sid: Order the mutton biryani, not chicken. Fight me. One plate feeds two freshers comfortably.

name: Ram Ki Bandi
description: A street cart that opens at 3 AM and has a queue by 3:15. Butter-drowned tawa dosas that ended the 'best dosa' debate years ago.
metro: Nampally (but you're going at 3 AM, so bike)
sid: The ultimate post-all-nighter quest. Finish an assignment, grab 3 friends, ride at 3 AM, eat cheese butter dosa, watch the city wake up. Core memory guaranteed.

name: Necklace Road
description: The lakeside boulevard around Hussain Sagar. The default night-drive loop — lights on the water, street food carts, open till late.
sid: Bike loop: Necklace Road → Tank Bund → back via NTR Marg. Do it once after your first internals to feel alive again.

name: Karachi Bakery (Moazzam Jahi)
description: The original 1953 branch inside the beautiful old Moazzam Jahi Market. Fruit biscuits that every Hyderabadi carries in their luggage.
bestTime: Any time; market is prettiest at dusk
sid: Buy fruit biscuits here before every trip home. Instant family hero. Also try the famous ice cream shops inside the same market.

name: Birla Mandir
description: White marble temple on a hill with a panoramic view of Hussain Sagar and the whole city glowing at night. Phones locked away — genuinely peaceful.
sid: No phones allowed inside and that's the best part. The city view at dusk hits different when you can't photograph it.

name: Ananthagiri Hills
description: The closest real forest to Hyderabad. Dense trails, sunrise viewpoints, coffee plantations. The standard first-year group-trip destination.
metro: None — bikes or a cab convoy
sid: The birthday-trip default. 8 people, 4 bikes, leave at 5, breakfast in Vikarabad, trek till noon, sleep the whole ride back. Peak first-year experience.

name: California Burrito — Gachibowli
description: The campus lifeline. Officially a Mexican fast-food chain, unofficially the fourth food group of IIITH. Closest branch to campus, hence permanently full of people you know.
sid: You will come here after every internals, every breakup, and every stipend credit. Budget accordingly. Burrito bowl + free sauce bar = optimal rupee-to-happiness ratio.

name: California Burrito — Madhapur
description: Same burritos, corpo crowd. This is where you eat when your internship's office is nearby and you want to feel something familiar among the lanyard people.
sid: Identical menu to Gachibowli but somehow tastes like ambition and deadline stress. Good Inorbit/cable-bridge combo stop.

name: California Burrito — Kondapur
description: The Sarath City–adjacent branch. For when you're at the mall, the food court queue is a war zone, and you remember burritos exist.
sid: Pro move: skip the Sarath City food court chaos entirely and eat here before the movie. Nachos travel well into a PVR if you're brave.

name: California Burrito — Financial District
description: The deep-south branch among the glass towers. Emptiest of the four, which means your order is ready before you finish saying 'extra chipotle'.
sid: The secret quiet one. Weekend bike ride + empty CalBur + no queue = the burrito speedrun any% world record attempt.

name: Inorbit Mall
description: The OG tech-crowd mall next to the cable bridge. Rooftop food court view of Durgam Cheruvu, solid brand lineup, and the default 'let's just go somewhere' answer for the Hitec side of the city.
sid: Combo move: Inorbit first, then walk to the cable bridge at sunset. Two quests, one trip.

name: Lulu Mall
description: The mega mall of the north — one of the biggest in the city. A food court the size of a football field, a hypermarket where you WILL get lost, and enough floors to count as cardio.
sid: Metro straight to KPHB and walk. Do NOT drive here on a weekend — the parking queue is longer than the movie.

name: GVK One
description: The classy compact mall on Road No.1. Smaller than the mega malls but posher — good restaurants, PVR, and Banjara Hills people-watching.
sid: Pair it with KBR park morning walk or Jubilee café hop. This is the 'parents are visiting' mall.

name: Sarath City Capital Mall
description: One of India's biggest malls, 10 minutes from campus. Gaming zone, bowling, food court the size of a stadium.
bestTime: Weekday evenings (weekends are a mosh pit)
sid: Birthday plan when nobody can decide: bowling + arcade + food court. Works every time. AMB Cinemas here is the fancy movie option.
